name: Trail of the Carreiro dos SS — circular route in the Serra de Montejunto
name_original: Trilho do Carreiro dos SS — percurso circular na Serra de Montejunto
region: Lamas e Cercal e Cercal e Cercal
region_intro: Hiking around Lamas e Cercal e Cercal e Cercal offers diverse trails primarily centered around the Serra de Montejunto. This limestone massif features elevations up to 666 meters, providing extensive panoramic views across the landscape. The terrain is characterized by holm oak and kermes oak vegetation, with historical sites such as the Real Fábrica do Gelo integrated into the hiking experience. The region's routes often include varied ascents and descents. Best hiking trails around Lamas e Cercal e Cercal e Cercal The most popular hiking route is Route of the Windmills, a 6.4 miles (10.2 km) trail that takes 3 hours 20 minutes to complete. This moderate route features significant elevation changes and views of historical windmills. Another top favourite among local hikers is Trilho da Quinta da Serra — circular route in Serra de Montejunto, a moderate 3.0 miles (4.9 km) path. This circular route explores the natural environment of Serra de Montejunto. Local hikers also love the Trail of the Carreiro dos SS — circular route in the Serra de Montejunto, a 5.3 miles (8.5 km) trail leading through the varied terrain of Serra de Montejunto, often completed in about 2 hours 45 minutes. Hiking around Lamas e Cercal e Cercal e Cercal is defined by limestone massifs, panoramic viewpoints, and historical ruins. The network offers options for different ability levels, from easy loops to more challenging moderate routes. The routes in Lamas e Cercal e Cercal e Cercal are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.8 stars from over 60 reviews. More than 200 hikers have used komoot to explore Lamas e Cercal e Cercal e Cercal's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many hiking trails are available in Lamas e Cercal e Cercal e Cercal?
A: The region offers a diverse selection of over 19 hiking trails. These include 5 easy routes, 13 moderate paths, and 1 more challenging trail, ensuring options for various fitness levels.

Q: Are there circular hiking routes in the area?
A: Yes, many of the trails in Lamas e Cercal e Cercal e Cercal are circular, allowing you to start and end at the same point. Examples include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1380477485/trilho-da-quinta-da-serra-circular-route-in-serra-de-montejunto" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Trilho da Quinta da Serra — circular route in Serra de Montejunto&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1380484809/trail-of-the-carreiro-dos-ss-circular-route-in-the-serra-de-montejunto" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Trail of the Carreiro dos SS — circular route in the Serra de Montejunto&lt;/a&gt;, both exploring the natural environment of Serra de Montejunto.

Q: What historical sites or attractions can I discover while hiking?
A: The trails in Lamas e Cercal e Cercal e Cercal often lead to fascinating historical sites. You can explore the ruins of the 18th-century &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/1760096" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Real Fábrica do Gelo&lt;/a&gt; (Royal Ice Factory) on Serra de Montejunto, which once supplied ice to the Portuguese Court. Additionally, the area features archaeological sites like Castro de Rocha Forte and the Grutas Necrópoles, offering glimpses into the region's past.

Q: Which trails offer the best panoramic views?
A: Many routes on the Serra de Montejunto provide extensive panoramic views due to its elevation of 666 meters. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1380443753/route-of-the-windmills" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Route of the Windmills&lt;/a&gt; is particularly noted for its significant elevation changes and scenic vistas across the landscape.

Q: Are there family-friendly hiking options in Lamas e Cercal e Cercal e Cercal?
A: Yes, the region offers several easy routes suitable for families. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/10344128" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Serra Montejunto Bar loop from Paisagem Protegida da Serra de Montejunto&lt;/a&gt; is an easy 3.5 km trail that provides a pleasant experience for all ages.

Q: What is the best time of year to go hiking in Lamas e Cercal e Cercal e Cercal?
A: The region is generally pleasant for hiking during spring and autumn when temperatures are milder. These seasons offer comfortable conditions to explore the limestone massifs and historical sites without the intense heat of summer or the potential for heavy winter rains.

Q: Can I hike with my dog in Lamas e Cercal e Cercal e Cercal?
A: Most outdoor areas in Portugal, including hiking trails, are generally dog-friendly, provided your pet is kept on a leash and you clean up after them. Always check local signage for any specific restrictions, especially in protected natural areas.

Q: What do other hikers enjoy the most about hiking in Lamas e Cercal e Cercal e Cercal?
A: The area is highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.8 stars from over 60 reviews. Reviewers often praise the diverse terrain, the stunning panoramic viewpoints from Serra de Montejunto, and the integration of historical ruins into the hiking experience.

Q: Are there any picnic areas or facilities along the trails?
A: Yes, you can find facilities such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/6301116" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Picnic Area of Serra de Montejunto&lt;/a&gt;, which provides a convenient spot to rest and enjoy a meal amidst nature. There is also the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5706330" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Montejunto Rural Campsite&lt;/a&gt; nearby.

Q: What kind of vegetation can I expect to see on the trails?
A: The dominant vegetation in the Serra de Montejunto area includes holm oak (azinheira) and kermes oak (carrasco). These species contribute to the distinct natural character of the region's landscapes.

Q: Are there any lakes or water features near the hiking routes?
A: While the immediate hiking trails are focused on the Serra de Montejunto, there are several lakes and water features in the broader vicinity. Notable highlights include &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/7152796" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Lagoa de Alcoentre&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5869247" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cabeço de Meca Quarry Lake&lt;/a&gt;, which can be explored as separate excursions.

Q: How challenging are the hikes in Lamas e Cercal e Cercal e Cercal?
A: The routes vary in difficulty. You'll find easy loops perfect for a leisurely stroll, as well as moderate routes with significant elevation changes, such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1380468447/trilho-dos-currais-e-calcada-circular-route-in-serra-de-montejunto" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Trilho dos Currais e Calçada — circular route in Serra de Montejunto&lt;/a&gt;, which involves notable ascents and descents. There is also one difficult trail for experienced hikers.
tips: Located in the chestnut woods, it connects the Real Fábrica do Gelo complex and the Environmental Interpretation Centre. The Park has eight tables, with an average capacity of six to eight people per table. As support structures for its use, it has a small bar, toilets and a small playground.

Located in the heart of the Montejunto mountain range, the Montejunto Rural Camping Park has ample shade and capacity for 90 people. Free to use, it has a bar, a tourist reception center and an Environmental Interpretation Centre.

https://montejunto.pt/parque-de-campismo-rural-da-serra-de-montejunto/

The karst landscape of Montejunto, in the extreme southwest of the Estremenho Limestone Massif, displays deep valleys, lages, gravel pits and lapiás, formed by aquatic erosion over geological eras. Freezing of water in rock fissures results in gravel deposits.

Located in Serra de Montejunto, Alenquer, the 13th century Chapel of São João Baptista is close to the Chapel of Nossa Senhora das Neves and the ruins of a Dominican monastery. Every year, on the 24th of June, a mass is held in honor of Saint John.

At the top of the Montejunto mountain range, very close to the chapel of Nossa Senhora das Neves, there are remains of the first convent of the Order of São Domingos in Portugal. Initially built in 1218, shortly after Friar Soeiro Gomes arrived in Portugal, the current ruins are the result of the rebuilding (over the initial convent) that took place in the 18th century, upon the return of the Dominicans. Despite being in ruins, it is a grandiose monument that must be visited, with due care, given the dangers that ruins always contain.

Source: Natural.pt

Next to the monument, there is a trail called "Calçada dos Frades" that runs along the slope. This cobbled path, which is also thought to be from the 18th century, was opened to serve the construction and use of the Convent of Nossa Senhora das Neves. The path was also used by the friars to access the Real Fabrica do Gelo, located further down, where today there is a laser area and an interpretive center.
